text: Introduce yourself as if you are accepting a lifetime achievement award.
Describe your morning routine to someone who has never woken up before.
Explain what you do for work to a curious 5-year-old.
Tell me about your favorite hobby and why it brings you joy.
Describe your ideal vacation destination without naming the place.
Persuade me that pizza is better than burgers. Give at least two reasons.
Tell a story about a time you got lost, with a surprising twist at the end.
Explain how to make your favorite meal, step by step.
Convince a skeptical friend to try meditation. Use personal benefits.
Describe a movie plot so badly that it becomes funny.
You are a tour guide showing aliens around Earth for the first time.
Defend the idea that remote work is more productive than office work.
Tell a fictional story that starts with: 'I never expected to find that in my attic.'
Explain blockchain technology to someone who thinks Bitcoin is a physical coin.
Persuade me that books are better than movies. Use three specific arguments.
You are a scientist who just discovered time travel. Explain your findings.
Argue that social media has done more harm than good to society.
Describe a character from a book as if they were applying for a job.
You are the CEO pitching your startup to investors in 90 seconds.
Tell a ghost story that takes place in an elevator.
Explain why failure is essential for success. Use historical examples.
You are a defense attorney giving closing arguments for an innocent client.
Describe color to someone who has been blind since birth.
Argue that artificial intelligence should have legal rights.
You are a teacher inspiring students on the first day of school.
Tell a story using only dialogue between two strangers on a train.
Convince your city council to build more parks instead of parking lots.
Explain quantum physics using only analogies from everyday life.
You are a motivational speaker at a graduation ceremony.
Argue that space exploration is a waste of money that should go to Earth.
Describe a world where humans can only communicate through music.
You are a journalist reporting live from a groundbreaking discovery.
Persuade someone to learn a new language. Share cognitive benefits.
Tell the story of humanity to an alien archaeologist 10,000 years from now.
You are a doctor explaining a diagnosis to a patient with empathy and clarity.
Debate: Should voting be mandatory in democracies? Argue the affirmative.
You are a coach giving a halftime speech to a team that is losing badly.
Explain the concept of empathy to someone who only understands logic.
You are a chef on a cooking show introducing your signature dish.
Argue that standardized testing is an outdated measure of intelligence.
Tell a story that begins with a lie but ends with an important truth.
You are a product manager launching a revolutionary new app.
Describe your biggest failure and what it taught you about resilience.
Explain climate change to someone who thinks it is a hoax. Be persuasive.
You are a detective presenting your theory at a murder mystery dinner.
Debate: Should college education be free for everyone? Argue yes.
You are a poet introducing your new collection at a literary festival.
Tell the story of an object in this room from its perspective.
You are a therapist giving advice to someone afraid of public speaking.
Argue that curiosity is more important than intelligence for success.
Introduce yourself at a networking event for industry leaders.
Describe the taste of chocolate to someone who has never eaten it.
You are a fitness trainer motivating a client who wants to give up.
Tell a true story about a random act of kindness you witnessed.
Explain the internet to someone from the 1800s.
You are a venture capitalist evaluating a startup pitch. Give your feedback.
Describe your dream house in vivid detail.
Argue that creativity cannot be taught, only nurtured.
You are a stand-up comedian doing your opening set.
Tell a story about a misunderstanding that led to something wonderful.
Explain why diversity in teams leads to better outcomes.
You are a museum curator introducing an exhibit about ancient civilizations.
Describe what happiness means to you without using the word 'happy'.
Argue that privacy is more important than security in the digital age.
You are a ship captain addressing your crew before a dangerous voyage.
Tell a science fiction story in exactly three sentences.
Explain how compound interest works using a real-world example.
You are a diplomat negotiating peace between two rival nations.
Describe your hometown to someone who will never visit it.
Argue that procrastination can sometimes be productive.
You are an astronaut sending a message back to Earth from Mars.
Tell the story of the worst advice you ever received and what happened.
Explain the concept of supply and demand using a lemonade stand.
You are a professor giving a lecture on the power of habits.
Persuade a book lover that audiobooks count as 'real' reading.
Describe a smell that instantly transports you to childhood.
You are a wedding officiant who has lost their notes. Improvise the ceremony.
Argue that travel is the best form of education.
Tell a story about an unexpected friendship between two opposites.
You are a tech support person explaining WiFi to someone who thinks it's magic.
Explain why sleep is the most underrated productivity tool.
You are a park ranger warning hikers about an incoming storm.
Describe the sound of your favorite song to a deaf person.
Argue that money can buy happiness, but only up to a point.
You are a survivor telling your story on a talk show.
Tell a story about finding something valuable that isn't material.
Explain how photosynthesis works to a curious middle schooler.
You are a conductor introducing a symphony to first-time concertgoers.
Persuade someone that failure is not the opposite of success but part of it.
Describe the feeling of accomplishing a long-term goal.
You are a whistleblower going public with an important revelation.
Argue that art should be funded by governments, not just private donors.
Tell a story about a day when everything went wrong but ended right.
You are a nutritionist debunking common diet myths.
Describe what courage looks like in everyday life.
You are a film director explaining your vision for an upcoming movie.
Persuade a skeptic that renewable energy is the only viable future.
Tell a story set in a world where lying is impossible.
You are a mentor giving advice to your younger self.
Give an impromptu leadership speech to a team facing a major crisis.
